FR-014.title: A line's add control becomes visible on keyboard focus and opens the composer when activated

FR-015.title: The Overview Review comments card uses the same state chip vocabulary and body-weight text

FR-016.title: On completed/archived specs annotations stay readable while mutating actions stay unavailable
tests: InlineComment.test.tsx::offers no actions at all on a read-only spec

intent: Make saved inline review comments read as quiet annotations on the line they describe, with pending/applied legible at a glance and refine/edit/delete one keyboard-reachable step away.
expectations: No review conversations: no replies, no resolve, no avatars, no Write/Preview composer
No change to how comments are stored, dispatched, or restored — the line-hover plus, the composer, the card, and the reviewComments path all stay
Refining a single comment in isolation (the hand-off stays document-level)
context: constraint: user comment text never interpolated into an HTML attribute via innerHTML
constraint: readable text uses --text-body/--text-primary, never the low-contrast secondary/muted tokens
constraint: quiet-surface vocabulary borrowed from the existing inline-code and .file-ref chip treatment
approach: Restyle the existing InlineComment into a collapsed, quiet annotation that expands in place on click/Enter/Space, borrowing the viewer's inline-code and file-chip surface vocabulary. Add an in-place edit via a new editComment message plus a pure editComment(ctx,id,text) helper alongside the existing add/remove/markApplied. Restore stops filtering applied comments so pending vs applied is legible on the line, while the Refine badge stays pending-only.

decisions.decision: Collapse the comment to one line and expand it in place via a real button with aria-expanded
decisions.why: Density is the complaint — a collapsed comment costs one line no matter how long it is, and a button gets the keyboard path, focus ring, and disclosure semantics for free
decisions.rejected: Hover-reveal (no keyboard path, undiscoverable) and a gutter marker plus side panel (decouples the comment from its line and duplicates the Overview list)

decisions.decision: Edit persists via a new additive editComment message and pure helper that mutates only the comment text
decisions.why: It keeps the comment identity, anchor, createdAt, and status, and rides the existing serialized comment-write queue
decisions.rejected: Delete-then-re-add, which mints a new id, drops createdAt, and resets an applied comment to pending

decisions.decision: Applied comments are restored inline in the quiet collapsed state but excluded from the Refine count
decisions.why: Pending vs applied cannot be legible at a glance if only one state ever appears on a line; the line becomes a record of what was asked
decisions.rejected: Keeping applied comments only in the Overview card

decisions.decision: Refine on an expanded pending comment dispatches the document batch via the existing runDocRefinement
decisions.why: Surfaces the hand-off at the comment without forking the dispatch semantics
decisions.rejected: Per-comment refinement dispatch — comments are handed to the AI as a document-level batch today

decisions.why: The Overview review card reuses .comment-text; a global truncation rule would have silently broken it — the card had no styles of its own and was inheriting the inline ones

decisions.decision: The document click-delegation handler ignores clicks inside .inline-comment
decisions.why: The Edit action opens the composer on the same click that bubbles to the close-on-outside-click handler, which would have closed it immediately
decisions.rejected: stopPropagation on the action buttons, which would have masked the ordering rather than stating the rule

decisions.decision: Extracted the completed/archived read-only check into one helper
decisions.why: The same fact was being re-derived in three places once the annotation needed it; two paths to one fact drift
decisions.rejected: A fourth inline copy of the dataset check

plan.summary: Presentation-and-disclosure pass over the existing inline comment, plus one additive edit mutation
plan.key_finding: The Refine badge is driven by the pendingRefinements signal, so applied comments must be mounted on their line without entering that signal

tasks.summary: 21 tasks across 8 phases — foundational types + edit mutation, then one phase per user story, then polish (tests, stories, docs)

implement.summary: Inline comments now rest as quiet one-line annotations that open on demand, distinguish pending from applied, and carry refine/edit/delete without a permanent delete control

T001.did: Added status to the webview Refinement type and the editComment message to ViewerToExtensionMessage

T002.did: Added the pure editComment(ctx,id,text) mutation preserving id/anchor/status/createdAt, no-op on blank or unknown id

T003.did: Routed the editComment message through the existing serialized comment-write queue

T004.did: Covered edit-in-place: text replaced, identity/anchor/status/createdAt preserved, blank and unknown-id no-ops

T005.did: Rebuilt InlineComment as a collapsed annotation — disclosure button with state glyph, truncated text, and state label, all text through Preact

T006.did: Restyled the annotation onto the viewer's quiet neutral surface — borderless, body-weight text, accent only on hover/focus, state rail, full truncation trio

T007.did: Carried each mounted comment's status through the add and restore paths into the annotation

T008.did: Added the disclosure and action row — aria-expanded/aria-controls, Refine only when pending, actions suppressed when read-only

T009.did: Gave the composer optional initialValue and submitLabel so edit reuses it pre-filled, with the caret at the end

T010.did: Opened the pre-filled composer for an existing comment and persisted the revision, keeping the click-outside handler from closing what Edit just opened

T011.did: Wired Edit and Refine into the mount path alongside delete, tracking mounted comments so applied ones can be acted on too

T012.did: Styled the expanded body and action row — body-weight text wrapping safely, ghost actions with an accent Refine and a red-on-hover Delete

T013.did: Restored applied comments inline alongside pending ones without entering the pending signal, so the Refine badge stays pending-only

T014.did: Styled the two states — accent rail plus comment glyph for pending, muted rail plus check glyph for applied

T015.did: Revealed the line add control on keyboard focus with a focus ring, closing the mouse-only gap

T016.did: Gave the Overview review list the annotation's state-chip vocabulary and body-weight text — its classes had no styles at all before

T017.did: Added component tests — collapsed/expanded, pending vs applied, refine hidden when applied, read-only, plus XSS regressions — and a restore-on-reopen suite covering drift, idempotence, and the pending-only Refine count

T018.did: Rewrote the stories for every state — pending collapsed/expanded, applied, read-only, long-text truncation, five-on-one-document density, markup-as-text, row mode — and added the composer's edit-mode story

T019.did: Described the annotation, its states, and its actions in the README's Reading Specs section

T021.did: Validated the Success Criteria — compile, 1503 tests, Storybook build, and vsix package all green

verified.result: 1503 passed / 111 suites — includes 18 new tests for the annotation, restore-on-reopen, and edit persistence
built — 8 InlineComment stories + the composer edit-mode story
